title: Alpine microhabitat richness phase
summary: Forty-ninth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-2 wave deepens treeline and tundra through one compact alpine microhabitat pack and one small alpine memory payoff using existing content-first surfaces.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the alpine microhabitat content pack.
execution_order.why_now: Treeline and tundra now want the same authored density the forest half has been getting.
A compact alpine pack can rebalance the biome chain without systems work.
The pack must stay science-safe and compact.

execution_order.goal: Prepare one compact alpine memory payoff.
execution_order.why_now: Once the content lands, one quiet payoff can make the alpine half more memorable.
The alpine half should feel remembered, not just fuller.
The payoff should stay compact and content-owned.

guardrails: Prefer a few strong alpine anchors over a broad species swell.
Reuse existing note, comparison, close-look, or sketchbook surfaces.
For the memory payoff, prefer the existing sketchbook seam over new comparison or close-look expansion.
Do not reopen route or traversal runtime.
acceptance_focus: Treeline and tundra feel denser and more distinct.
The alpine half gains one compact memory payoff.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-2.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-alpine-microhabitat-richness-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 2's next wave: deepen treeline and tundra through one compact alpine microhabitat pack and one small memory payoff without reopening route or traversal systems.
